qa_pairs.question: What is the default network setting for an Arbutus project when launching a virtual machine?
qa_pairs.answer: On Arbutus, you should select your project network by default, which usually starts with 'def-project-name'.

qa_pairs.question: Should I modify the Network Ports settings when launching a VM?
qa_pairs.answer: No, you should not change the Network Ports settings at this time.

qa_pairs.question: How do I ensure the default security group is allocated for my VM?
qa_pairs.answer: The default security group should be in the 'Allocated' list. If it is not, move it from 'Available' to 'Allocated' using the upwards arrow on the far right of the group's row.

qa_pairs.question: How do I select an SSH key pair for my virtual machine?
qa_pairs.answer: From the 'Available' list under the 'Key Pair' section, select the SSH key pair you created earlier by clicking the upwards arrow on the far right of its row.

qa_pairs.question: What if I don't have an SSH key pair when launching a VM?
qa_pairs.answer: If you do not have a key pair, you can create or import one from the 'Key Pair' window using the buttons at the top of the window.

qa_pairs.question: What should I do with the Configuration, Server Groups, Scheduler Hints, and Metadata settings during VM creation?
qa_pairs.answer: You should not change these settings at this time.

qa_pairs.question: What happens after I click the 'Launch Instance' button?
qa_pairs.answer: After clicking the 'Launch Instance' button, your virtual machine will be created. The list of instances will be displayed, and the 'Task' field will initially show 'Spawning'. Once the VM has spawned, its power state will be 'Running', which may take a few minutes.

qa_pairs.question: What types of IP addresses can a virtual machine have?
qa_pairs.answer: Each VM will have at least one private IP address, and some may also have a second public IP assigned to it.

qa_pairs.question: What is the purpose of a private IP address for a VM?
qa_pairs.answer: A private IP address provides inter-VM networking within the project but does not allow for connection to the outside world. Any VM in your project will have a private IP address of the form 192.168.X.Y.

qa_pairs.question: What is the purpose of a public IP address for a VM?
qa_pairs.answer: Public IPs allow outside services and tools to initiate contact with your VM, enabling you to connect to it from your personal computer for administrative tasks or to serve web content. Public IPs can also be pointed to by domain names.

qa_pairs.question: How do I assign a public IP address to my virtual machine?
qa_pairs.answer: On the 'Instances' list, click the drop-down arrow (▼) for your VM and select 'Associate Floating IP'. In the 'Allocate Floating IP' window, click 'Allocate IP'. If it's your first time, click the '+' sign in the 'Manage Floating IP Associations' dialog box first. Then, click the 'Associate' button.

qa_pairs.question: How can I verify that my VM has a public IP address?
qa_pairs.answer: After assigning, you should see two IP addresses in your 'IP Address' column: one of the form 192.168.X.Y (private) and the other your public IP. You can also find a list of public IP addresses and their associated projects by going to 'Network->Floating IPs'.
